name: K.U.M.C.
description_short: NCUA-insured credit union headquartered in Shawnee, Kansas, offering savings accounts, CDs, money market accounts, and home equity products through 4 branch locations.
description_long:
K.U.M.C. Credit Union was established in 1954 and operates as a federally insured financial institution regulated by the NCUA. Based in Shawnee, Kansas, the credit union has grown to approximately $29 million in assets and serves its membership through a network of 4 branch locations. The institution maintains a physical headquarters at 12510 W 62nd Ter Ste 104, Shawnee, KS 66216.

K.U.M.C. offers a standard suite of retail banking products including savings accounts (0.05% APY with $2,500 minimum), certificates of deposit with terms ranging from 6 months to 36 months (0.60% to 0.90% APY), and money market accounts (0.20% to 0.40% APY depending on balance tier). As a credit union, the institution also facilitates access to home equity products, though these are offered through partner lenders rather than directly underwritten by K.U.M.C.

The credit union distinguishes itself through NCUA insurance protection on member deposits, a long operational history spanning over 70 years, and multiple physical branch locations for in-person service. The website provides transparent rate disclosures updated regularly and emphasizes confirming current rates directly before visiting branches, indicating operational transparency about rate changes.

K.U.M.C. represents a traditional community credit union model with modest asset size and limited geographic footprint. Deposit rates are comparable to or slightly below national averages for credit unions. The institution's home equity offerings are advertiser-sponsored rather than proprietary products, limiting direct control over lending terms. The 4-branch operational model may limit accessibility for consumers outside the Kansas City metropolitan area. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Money market accounts tiered at 0.20%, 0.20%, and 0.40% APY
Home equity line of credit access through partner lenders
Account opening at physical branch locations (4 total locations)
pros: NCUA federal insurance protection on deposits up to applicable limits
Long-established institution founded in 1954 with 70+ years of operational history
Multiple physical branch locations (4 branches) for in-person account services
CD rates up to 0.90% APY for 36-month terms with various minimum options
Transparent rate disclosure with regular updates (last reported April 5, 2026)
Money market accounts tiered by balance with rates up to 0.40% APY
Bilingual website support (English and Spanish) for member accessibility
cons: Savings account APY of only 0.05% is significantly below current market rates for high-yield savings accounts (typically 4-5%)
Limited geographic reach with only 4 branches concentrated in Kansas area, restricting convenience for non-local customers
Modest $29 million in total assets suggests limited lending capacity and fewer product innovations compared to larger institutions
CD rates (0.60%-0.90%) lag behind many online banks and credit unions offering 4-5% rates on comparable terms
Home equity products are partner-lender sponsored rather than directly offered, reducing control over terms and potentially limiting member-specific pricing
best_for: Kansas City metropolitan area residents seeking in-person credit union banking services
Conservative savers prioritizing NCUA insurance protection and traditional banking relationships over maximum deposit rates
Members seeking short-term CD products with flexible minimum deposit options ranging from $10,000 to $100,000
Community-focused consumers preferring local credit union membership over national bank accounts
diagnosis: K.U.M.C. is best suited for Kansas-area residents seeking traditional credit union banking with federal insurance protection and branch accessibility, or conservative savers willing to accept below-market deposit rates in exchange for community banking relationships. Primary caveat: deposit rates are substantially below online banking alternatives, and limited branch footprint restricts geographic accessibility for non-local customers.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
